danger_signals.visual_form: Sprite animations of Goombas, Koopa Troopas, Piranha Plants, Bullet Bills, Spinies, Bloopers, Hammers
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate collision hazard; defeat by jumping on or using Fire Flower

danger_signals.visual_form: Screen edge drop-off or visual absence of solid ground extending downward
danger_signals.meaning: Instant death if player falls; requires precision platforming

danger_signals.visual_form: Animated red/orange terrain in later worlds (8-x), firebars rotating through space
danger_signals.meaning: Contact causes immediate damage or death; must avoid or use invincibility

danger_signals.visual_form: Mario sprite is half-height compared to Super Mario form
danger_signals.meaning: One hit from any enemy or hazard causes death; vulnerable state

danger_signals.visual_form: Countdown display at top of screen reaching zero
danger_signals.meaning: Time-pressure hazard; reaching zero results in instant death

danger_signals.visual_form: Plant sprite rising from pipe in synchronized pattern
danger_signals.meaning: Timed hazard that appears predictably; must time movement to avoid

danger_signals.visual_form: Animated rotating bars of fire projectiles around fixed points
danger_signals.meaning: High-damage obstacle requiring spatial timing to navigate through gaps

safety_signals.visual_form: Red/white mushroom sprite emerging from ? block
safety_signals.meaning: Grants size upgrade; provides one damage absorption buffer

safety_signals.visual_form: Animated flower sprite (red/yellow) emerging from ? block
safety_signals.meaning: Grants projectile attack capability; expands offensive options

safety_signals.visual_form: Flashing star sprite with rotating animation
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary invincibility period; allows collision with enemies without harm

safety_signals.visual_form: Green mushroom with distinct spotting pattern
safety_signals.meaning: Grants extra life; hidden in bricks as fail-safe resource
Safe traversal zone; supports Mario movement without hazard

safety_signals.visual_form: Small circular glinting sprite in midair or blocks
safety_signals.meaning: Resource collection; 100 coins grants extra life

safety_signals.visual_form: Red/white flagpole structure at level end with flag attachment
safety_signals.meaning: Goal completion; touching flag lowers it and triggers level victory

faction_indicators.color_identity: Red and brown (shirt and overalls in sprite)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: M on cap (sprite detail); mushroom motif aligns with protagonist
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Footprint trail animation during movement; distinct silhouette

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: L on cap (sprite detail); visually distinguished from Mario only by color swap
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Identical movement and interaction patterns to Mario; functionally equivalent character

faction_indicators.color_identity: Dark (black outline sprite) with red accents (fire breathing)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Crown visible on sprite; large turtle-like silhouette; castle fortress association
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Bowser appears only as level 8-4 boss; no standard enemy variant
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Castle structure with dark stone aesthetic; fire/lava surrounding boss arena

faction_indicators.color_identity: Earth tones (browns, greens); Toads depicted in red/white mushroom colors
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Princess Toadstool (rescue target); mushroom-shaped Toad NPCs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Castle ruins scattered throughout; flag markers; green pipe structures (warp infrastructure)

faction_indicators.color_identity: Red and green variants for different unit types (red = stationary, green = mobile)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Turtle/shell silhouette; retractable shell behavior as faction signature
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Red Koopa Troopa (slower, land-based), Green Koopa Troopa (faster, land-based), Koopa Paratroopa (winged variants of both colors)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Shell projectiles; organized placement in later levels suggesting military positioning

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mushroom-like cap silhouette; simple enemy design suggests low-level unit status
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Standard brown Goomba only; no visual sub-types
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Paired/grouped placement in early levels; frequent spawn points suggest basic mook designation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Green grass/trees, blue sky, brown earth terrain, abundant platforms, visible coins, few hazards
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Low enemy density, predictable Goomba/Koopa Troopa encounters, straightforward level design, generous checkpoint positioning

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Introduction of water/cave tiles, darker backgrounds, increased spike/firebar placement, narrower passages
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased enemy aggression, introduction of Piranha Plants and Spinies, more complex platforming sequences

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Lava/fire tiles prominent, dark red/orange color scheme, castle fortress visual progression, dense enemy spacing
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: High difficulty enemy placement, Bullet Bill and Hammer Brother encounters, minimal platform safety, time pressure intensified

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gray stone textures, dark backgrounds, pipe entry visual, dungeon aesthetic appears
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Level architecture shifts to enclosed spaces, reduced visibility, increased reliance on enemy patterns rather than navigation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mario sprite shrinks visually; audio cue triggers; brief invincibility frames with sprite flashing
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Next collision is fatal; player behavior changes to avoidance-focused rather than aggressive; risk perception increases

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mario sprite rotates/flashes with rapid animation; distinct audio loop plays continuously
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player behavior shifts to offensive (jump through enemies); enemy collision no longer causes fear response; time-limited window creates urgency

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mario sprite holds fire flower graphic; fireball projectiles visible when attack input given
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player gains ranged attack option; engagement distance increases; fire hazard avoidance becomes secondary concern

environmental_storytelling.example: Grass/sky world → underground cave → castle hierarchy communicates player advancing toward Bowser's seat of power
Early levels feature Goombas on flat ground; later levels introduce lava pits and fire obstacles, visually demonstrating increasing danger as player approaches endgame
Simple structures early (grass platforms) evolve into fortress stone architecture, signaling player entering enemy stronghold
Red/orange lava world signals danger through warm color palette; blue water world signals different hazard type through cool palette
Sparse Goomba placement in early levels teaches enemy patterns; dense enemy clusters in later levels demonstrate escalating territorial control by antagonists
Abundance of mushrooms and flowers in early levels suggests Mushroom Kingdom's resources; scarcity in later levels implies Bowser's occupation depletes defenses

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Walks in straight line; reverses direction on wall/edge collision; deterministic movement pattern
Sprite compresses/flattens; disappears; score points awarded; communication of enemy defeat
Walks slowly in consistent direction; shell visible; relatively stationary threat
Walks faster than red variant; indicates mobile patrol unit through speed differentiation
Sprite retracts into shell; shell becomes projectile entity; player can redirect threat as weapon
Animated rise/fall pattern from pipe opening; synchronized bite animation; time-based hazard
Continuous rotation around fixed pivot point; predictable trajectory; navigable through timing
Fast horizontal projectile movement; unstopping trajectory; represents high-damage ranged threat
Breathing fire projectiles; walking pattern; castle bridge arena; final antagonist encounter; sprite size indicates power/importance
Size change (Mushroom) or sprite modification (Fire Flower); communication of mechanical state change through immediate visual feedback
Sprite flashing animation; unique audio loop; behavioral freedom to collide with enemies without consequence

notes: Super Mario Bros. uses classical platformer visual language: color as faction identity (red Mario vs. red/green Koopas), sprite size differentiation for threat hierarchy, environmental aesthetics to signal danger progression (grass→cave→lava→castle), and animation states to communicate entity behavior and world-state changes. The game establishes core paradigms for platformer manifestation that persist across the genre: hazard signals through distinct sprite design, safety through power-up visual distinctness, and progression through environmental visual escalation. No complex political systems exist; threat structure is purely antagonist (Bowser faction) vs. protagonist (Mario/Luigi). The visual communication relies entirely on sprite animation, color identity, and level architecture rather than UI elements or dialogue.
